feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Peru race tightens again amid market volatility as overseas, rural ballots pour in
entry.content: Peru race tightens again amid market volatility as overseas, rural ballots pour in

entry.title: Boy, bye: Ariana Grande and Ethan Slater reportedly broke up several months ago
entry.content: 'Wicked' co-stars Ariana Grande and Ethan Slater have reportedly split after three years together. The 'quiet breakup' happened 'several months ago.'

entry.title: Longtime Epstein assistant paints late sex offender as master manipulator and denies knowing about his crimes
entry.content: Longtime Epstein assistant paints late sex offender as master manipulator and denies knowing about his crimes

entry.title: Spotify accused of using new streaming policy to reduce indie artist's royalties
entry.content: Mark Kratter, a Connecticut-based musician, is suing Spotify for engaging in what he alleges are "unfair business practices." The complaint claims that streaming metrics have been altered to prioritize major artists and labels above smaller indie musicians.

entry.title: Modest Mouse deals with death and breathes life into a new album that 'actually is ours'
entry.content: Three decades after Modest Mouse's scrappy Northwest beginnings, Isaac Brock returns with "An Eraser And A Maze," a new album he says finally feels wholly theirs.

entry.title: Questlove's Earth, Wind & Fire doc shows how trauma lingered behind a band's relentless positivity
entry.content: Drummer, DJ and director Ahmir "Questlove" Thompson talks about how the funky but aspirational band was much more than its biggest hit; plus, three other documentaries to watch in June.

entry.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
entry.content: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

entry.title: U.S. Apache helicopter shot down by Iran, Trump says; crew rescued by sea drone
entry.content: The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone in the first such operation ever carried out by the U.S. military, officials told CBS News.

entry.title: When should you repair an AC unit instead of buying a new one?
entry.content: Repairing your AC can save you thousands of dollars, but only if you know when fixing it makes financial sense.

entry.title: NASA names 4 astronauts for next Artemis mission
entry.content: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

entry.title: Trump says U.S. must respond after Apache helicopter attack over Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: President Trump said on social media that the U.S. "must respond" to the attack on an Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean has more.

entry.title: Trump says Iran shot down U.S. Apache helicopter, crew rescued by sea drone
entry.content: President Trump is vowing to respond after confirming that Iran shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone, the first rescue of its kind. CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.

entry.title: Steve Martin, Ann Philbin to curate new Santa Barbara Museum of Art exhibit of 'peculiar' paintings
entry.content: 'Martin Mull: The Joys of Indoor/Outdoor Living' in Santa Barbara will be curated by comedian-actor Steve Martin and former Hammer Museum director Ann Philbin.

entry.title: EU orders Meta to restore WhatsApp access for rival AI chatbots
entry.content: European Union regulators have ordered Meta Platforms to restore access to WhatsApp for rival AI chatbot makers

entry.title: Over $500,000 worth of bourbon stolen in daylight warehouse heist
entry.content: Police are looking for a gang of thieves who stole more than 10,000 bottles of bourbon from a Philadelphia warehouse.

entry.title: MIT researchers channel AI to turn hand gestures into robot training data
entry.content: Humanoid robots struggling with tasks like grasping a cup have a new teacher — a person wearing an ultrasound wristband that captures the movement of muscles, tendons and ligaments beneath the skin

entry.title: This rural California county’s problem is all too common: how to reduce suicides when everyone has a gun
entry.content: Shasta county has one of the state’s highest rates of suicide and gun ownership. Here’s how locals are trying to combat itLike many men in the mountainous California county of Shasta, about 200 miles north of San Francisco, Bill Rocha loved to hunt and fish, spending the infernal summers out on the lake in his boat. For decades he made his living as a contractor, working hard with his hands every day. And like many men in rural parts of the state, Bill was a gun owner. He had several hunting rifles, some of which he kept locked in a safe, and another firearm that he kept unlocked in his car.Kelly Rocha, his daughter, described him as extremely sociable, but in private things were starting to fray. She didn’t know the extent of what her father was struggling with until she got a call one night in 2019. She had slept through two voicemails from her father’s wife, but finally picked up when her own mother called. “It was after midnight,” recalled Kelly, who was then 43. “She told me that my dad went out to his truck and killed himself.” Continue reading...

entry.title: Federal appeals court says Alabama nitrogen gas execution inflicts unconstitutional suffering
entry.content: A federal appeals court ruled Monday that Alabama's nitrogen gas execution method likely inflicts a cruel and constitutionally impermissible degree of suffering on condemned inmates -- but stopped short of blocking a scheduled execution set for Thursday.

entry.title: Whey protein shortage looms as use of weight-loss drugs fuels global demand
entry.content: Price of dairy product has risen fivefold after users of GLP-1 medications advised to increase protein consumptionThe growing popularity of weight-loss drugs has fuelled global demand for whey protein, sparking concerns among industry experts over a potential shortage.The price of whey has risen fivefold to record levels as companies race to secure supplies amid a boom driven by growing use of GLP-1 drugs, such as Mounjaro, which often require higher protein intake to preserve muscle mass. Continue reading...

entry.title: Which Stock Sectors Do Best When Interest Rates Are Rising, Falling or Flat?
entry.content: Research finds a flat environment is best for stocks of all stripes, but there are some surprising results when the Fed is cutting or raising rates.

entry.title: Apple and Brussels blame each other for delaying European Union rollout of Siri AI
entry.content: Apple and the European Union are blaming each other for delaying the rollout of Apple's Siri AI app to European users

entry.title: ‘Woefully unprepared’: extreme heat will double US hospitalizations by 2040, study finds
entry.content: Sharp rise in hospital visits will in turn drive up annual healthcare costs for heat-related conditions to over $1bnPeople in the US are poised to endure another summer of unusually ferocious heat and there will be little respite in the years ahead, with a new study finding that the coming 15 years could see a doubling in hospitalizations due to heat-related illnesses.The number of annual heat-related emergency department visits or hospitalizations across the US are set to rise from about 109,000 cases a year to as many as 237,000 cases by 2040, the new research has estimated. Continue reading...

entry.title: Wyoming’s Data-Center Boom Meets the ‘Man Camp’ Backlash
entry.content: In America’s least populous state, neighbors are in an uproar over plans to build a small city to house thousands of out-of-state workers.

entry.title: Drug suspects arrested after vehicle in high-speed chase hits pedestrians
entry.content: One suspect drove a vehicle "at very high speed and recklessly, even hitting several local residents" before being stopped by authorities, officials said.

entry.title: Teen charged over London arson attack on Jewish charity ambulances
entry.content: 18-year-old Subhan Ahmed allegedly assisted with the torching of four ambulances used by a Jewish volunteer organization

entry.title: Iran and Israel say attacks halted after Trump tells both to "stop 'shooting'"
entry.content: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.

entry.title: Trump says Iran deal could be signed in 2 or 3 days
entry.content: Trump voices optimism for a "very good deal" with Iran in just a few days, as a tense truce between Israel and Iran appears to hold.

entry.title: 2 pilots killed in plane crash en route to pick up former MLB star
entry.content: MLB All-Star former catcher Yadier Molina said on social media that the plane was bound for Texas to pick him up, along with family and friends.

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama, Spurs send boisterous Knicks fans home quiet with Game 3 win
entry.content: New York Knicks fans rained vulgarities down on Victor Wembanayma and his teammates inside Madison Square Garden but the San Antonio Spurs responded with their best game of the NBA Finals.

entry.title: Platner seeks Maine Senate nomination and more primaries to watch today
entry.content: Maine Democrat Graham Platner is seeking to make the nomination official to take on Republican Sen. Susan Collins in November.

entry.title: Chrysler recalls more than 1 million Jeeps it says could catch fire
entry.content: Chrysler is recalling almost 1.08 million Jeep Wranglers and Gladiators it says could catch fire even when they're parked and turned off, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration​.

entry.title: A Rush to Stockpile Oil Will Keep Prices Higher for Longer
entry.content: Countries will build larger emergency crude reserves to reduce their vulnerability to future energy shocks.

entry.title: Inside Google’s AI training for teachers
entry.content: Google flew 70 teachers and administrators to its California headquarters to learn about its AI products — and how to persuade skeptical colleagues to use them.

entry.title: Graham Platner and big races for governor: What to watch in Tuesday’s primaries
entry.content: Four states vote Tuesday, with Maine’s key Senate race capturing the most national attention and Trump picking sides in South Carolina’s race for governor.

entry.title: U.N. court chief prosecutor suspended over sexual misconduct allegations
entry.content: Embattled International Criminal Court chief prosecutor Karim Khan was suspended after the court's oversight body referred him for disciplinary proceedings.

entry.title: Sweden set to ban mobile phones in schools, joining trend of shelving screens for students
entry.content: Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools from the next academic year as part of a broad reversal on the use of screens in classrooms

entry.title: Stock Market Today: Stocks Continue Recovery
entry.content: Nasdaq futures gain, with oil slightly lower

entry.title: U.S. pilots rescued after helicopter goes down near Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: A two-person U.S. military crew was rescued after their helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz. The cause of the incident remains unknown. President Trump told reporters that “the pilots are fine,” and that no one was injured in the incident.

entry.title: Healthy cooperation: how northern universities are linking with NHS trusts to drive innovation
entry.content: Backed by a mix of private and public finance, Huddersfield and Manchester are among many in the academic sector helping to create jobs and growthHuddersfield might appear an unlikely setting for a thriving health research complex. The West Yorkshire town is best known for its manufacturing heritage, but has quickly become a honey pot for private sector businesses keen to collaborate with the town’s university in a push for the latest medical breakthroughs.Next month, the driving force behind the University of Huddersfield’s national health innovation campus, Prof Liz Towns-Andrews, expects to get the go-ahead for the third of seven planned eco-buildings for research and tech development clustered near the town centre. Continue reading...

entry.title: Mike Brown is unhappy with the lopsided officiating in Game 3 of the NBA Finals
entry.content: Game 3 of the 2026 NBA Finals was quite competitive, but it seems Knicks coach Mike Brown was unhappy with how the referees took control.

entry.title: Luis Rengifo struggling with new club, could have short stay with Brewers
entry.content: Is Milwaukee's third baseman playing himself into being a top candidate to be designated for assignment?

entry.title: Where Victor Wembanyama's big Game 3 ranks in Madison Square Garden's NBA Finals history
entry.content: The NBA Finals have been to Madison Square Garden six times, but Victor Wembanyama turned in one of the best performances the arena has seen with a title on the line.

entry.title: Mike Brown calls out Knicks-Spurs Game 3 officiating: 'Never thought I'd see that in an NBA Finals game'
entry.content: New York's head coach had something to say about the officials after his team's first loss in over a month.

entry.title: Oilers' Sebastian Cossa trade rumor isn't what it seems
entry.content: There are reasons this would make a lot of sense.

entry.title: Mitch Johnson and the Spurs are not worried about Victor Wembanyama's motivation
entry.content: Despite being only in his third season, the Spurs know they can always rely on Victor Wembanyama to be impactful.

entry.title: Who is Sophie Jovanovic? How female goalie makes OHL history with Brentford Bulldogs draft pick
entry.content: This is an astounding moment in the OHL.

entry.title: Bills' Josh Allen wants to bring Buffalo a Super Bowl, and 23-25 Jets coach somehow mocks him
entry.content: Allen wants the Lombardi Trophy for Western New York.

entry.title: ‘You’re treated like this is the end’: Meet the dementia rebels – diagnosed and determined to change people’s minds
entry.content: Few things are more feared than a dementia diagnosis. Now people living with the condition are fighting against damaging stereotypes and demanding proper medical supportWhen Maxine Linnell, 78, a retired psychotherapist living in Leicestershire, learned that she had dementia four years ago, the diagnosis proved less challenging than some people’s reactions. “What was striking was how many people’s attitudes changed almost immediately … they stop seeing you as a person and see only dementia, some professionals included. Like this is the end and everything after will be devastating.”The assumption that you go overnight from diagnosis to late-stage dementia isn’t confined to family and friends. Julie Hayden, a nurse and social worker from Yorkshire, was diagnosed nine years ago at the age of 54, long after sensing that something was wrong but being constantly told that it was depression or menopause; her doctors still associated dementia with old age and didn’t consider that she might have had young onset. “At the point of diagnosis,” she recalls, “most of us are told: ‘Well, it’s dementia, nothing we can do about that. Best go away and get your end of life affairs in order.’” Continue reading...

entry.title: Instagram finally lets you reorder posts on your grid
entry.content: You can now rearrange posts on Instagram the way you want to.

entry.title: The criminal cartels cashing in on the World Cup – podcast
entry.content: Football fans are celebrating the tournament coming to Guadalajara. But with a brutal crime syndicate holding sway there, what are the risks for fans – and the government?Excitement is mounting in Mexico as the World Cup opens in Mexico City, then heads to the city of Guadalajara.Mexican journalist Leon Krauze is a fan. He was there the last time the World Cup came to Mexico and will be watching again. The city of Guadalajara has a mythical footballing past: “Pele’s Brazil played there in 1970, then Zico and Socrates played there in 1986. There is a real football memory there, a love affair between Guadalajara and football in general, and I expect it to be a wonderful party.” Continue reading...

entry.title: Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
entry.content: Can Apple's new AI glow up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?

entry.title: OpenAI files confidential SEC paperwork for IPO
entry.content: OpenAI has filed preliminary paperwork to potentially become a publicly traded company

entry.title: Wall Street Is Rushing to Fund the AI Bonanza in Every Conceivable Way
entry.content: From giant debt deals to IPOs, tech companies keep raking in investor cash.

entry.title: Mercor’s Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of ‘dual-pricing’ valuation tricks
entry.content: Sequoia is just one of the top firms that sells same equity at two different prices.

entry.title: Spencer Pratt Fails to Advance in Los Angeles Mayoral Race
entry.content: Incumbent Karen Bass and progressive Nithya Raman edged out the former reality-TV star.

entry.title: The Bitter Feud Between a Dog Walker and a Dentist Over Who Owns the Beach
entry.content: A lakefront homeowner likened his neighbor’s shoreline walks to a home invasion in a dispute that could go to the Wisconsin Supreme Court.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
To avoid criticism, do nothing, say nothing, be nothing.
Lucky Numbers: 57, 88, 1, 58, 91, 41

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Keep ginger in your freezer to make it last longer.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Wearing headphones for just an hour will increase the bacteria in your ear by 700 times.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — William Faulkner
entry.content: “Given the choice between the experience of pain and nothing, I would choose pain.” — William Faulkner

entry.title: The iOS 27 beta pretty much confirms that an Apple foldable is happening
entry.content: Can't a multi-trillion-dollar company have secrets any more?

entry.title: 'I was a prisoner in my home': Drug gangs 'cuckooing' hundreds of homes a week, police warn
entry.content: 'I was a prisoner in my home': Drug gangs 'cuckooing' hundreds of homes a week, police warn

entry.title: Meta quietly removes face-recognition code from its smart glasses app
entry.content: The 'disappearing into the bushes like Homer Simpson' strategy is a bold choice.

entry.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
entry.content: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

entry.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
entry.content: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

entry.title: Sam Bankman-Fried formally applies for a pardon
entry.content: The FTX CEO was sentenced for fraud in 2024.

entry.title: Bandits in north-west Nigeria abduct villagers they invited to discuss peace talks
entry.content: Thirty-nine people taken near Magamin Diddi village in Maradun municipality, north-west Zamfara state, police sayArmed bandits in north-west Nigeria abducted dozens of villagers whom they invited to a meeting about potential peace negotiations, authorities and residents said on Monday, highlighting the region’s worsening security.According to local police, 39 people were seized on Sunday during a meeting in the forest near Magamin Diddi village in the Maradun municipality of north-west Zamfara state. But some residents and officials believe the number of those abducted could be as high as 50. Continue reading...

entry.title: Spurs close in on Man City's Savinho - Tuesday's gossip
entry.content: Spurs make progress with negotiations to sign Manchester City winger Savinho, Manchester United are battling their city rivals for Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson, Atletico Madrid and Barcelona eye Chelsea full-back Marc Cucurella, plus more.

entry.title: Braves predicted to part ways with underperforming $20M Gold Glove winner at the trade deadline
entry.content: The Atlanta Braves are the best team in baseball and this big offseason signing is underperforming. He has been the worst Braves player by far and it is expected they trade him at some point.

entry.title: Micah Parsons picking Caleb Williams as NFL’s best player is way too fishy
entry.content: Micah Parsons is up to something. Caleb Williams and the Bears better be careful.

entry.title: “He Finally Won a Bet”: NFL Fans React as Brendan Sorsby Gets Cleared for the 2026 College Football Season
entry.content: Texas Tech Red Raiders quarterback Brendan Sorsby was granted a temporary injunction against the NCAA on Monday. It allows him to play in the 2026 season, after a legal battle regarding his eligibility. NEWS: A judge in district court in Lubbock County, Texas, has granted the injunction requested by Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby. […] The post “He Finally Won a Bet”: NFL Fans React as Brendan Sorsby Gets Cleared for the 2026 College Football Season appeared first on Gridiron Heroics.

entry.title: Insider Thinks It Would Be 'Surprise' If Christian Gonzalez Does Not Report To Camp
entry.content: The New England Patriots start mandatory minicamp on Tuesday, and Christian Gonzalez's attendance will be something to keep an eye on.

entry.title: Ja Morant's best landing spot isn't what you expect
entry.content: An unexpected trade proposal sends Grizzlies star Ja Morant to the Timberwolves for Julius Randle and Donte DiVincenzo.

entry.title: Behind the scenes look at A.J. Brown's first media day with Patriots
entry.content: The New England Patriots' social media team shared a sneak peek behind the scenes at A.J. Brown's media video shoot.

entry.title: Following Anthropic, OpenAI files confidentially for IPO
entry.content: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

entry.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
entry.content: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

entry.title: Google cuts the price of its AI Plus plan and doubles the storage
entry.content: The AI subscription now starts at $5 per month.

entry.title: Apple is dropping support for some pretty modern Watch models
entry.content: Three smartwatch models from 2022 and 2023 won't run watchOS 27.

entry.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
entry.content: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.

entry.title: Waymo bought Apple’s self-driving car proving ground for $220M
entry.content: Waymo has acquired a massive 5,500-acre proving ground in Arizona owned by Route 14 Investment Partners LLC, a Delaware shell company associated with Apple, according to documents filed with Maricopa County.

entry.title: Man who killed O'Shae Sibley after he danced at a Brooklyn gas station is convicted of manslaughter
entry.content: A jury convicted a man of manslaughter as a hate crime on Monday in the death of O'Shae Sibley, who was killed at a Brooklyn gas station during a confrontation that began with a group of young people shouting racist and anti-gay slurs at the professional dancer and his friends as they vogued to a Beyonce song.

entry.title: Chip Stocks Lead a Market Rebound
entry.content: Plus, Apple unveils an AI-enhanced Siri, and SpaceX’s IPO “pop”.

entry.title: iOS 27 can run on phones as old as the iPhone 11
entry.content: Apple's focus on efficiency and performance improvements is good news for older iPhones.

entry.title: Man in Texas is accused of using a fake boarding pass to get on a United flight to Los Angeles
entry.content: A Texas man is accused of using a fake boarding pass to get on a United Airlines flight and causing the plane to go back to the gate at Houston’s George Bush Intercontinental Airport

entry.title: Microsoft’s open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
entry.content: Microsoft shut down dozens of GitHub code repositories for Azure and AI coding tools after a reported hack.

entry.title: 📸 Cody Gakpo hits two Dutch milestones in warm up clash with Uzbekistan 👏
entry.content: After a shock loss to Algeria ended their 10-game unbeaten run and the sad news of Jurrien Timber being ruled out of the World Cup, the Netherlands needed an uplift today. Thanks to Cody Gakpo, they ...

entry.title: Todd Golden, Florida eyeing 5-star 2027 point guard Cayden Daughtry
entry.content: Florida basketball has amped up recruitment for MaxPreps National Junior of the Year, 5-star point guard Cayden Daughtry.

entry.title: Where to watch New York Yankees vs. Cleveland Guardians: Live stream, start time, TV channel, odds for Monday, June 8
entry.content: The Yankees start a three-game series with the Guardians.

entry.title: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting Report: Bear Alexander, DL, Oregon
entry.content: Bear Alexander has been in college football for several seasons now and he is poised for his biggest season yet.

entry.content: We’re now under two months out from the MLB trade deadline and there’s been increasing buzz surrounding big names like […]

entry.title: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting Report: A'Mauri Washington, DL, Oregon
entry.content: A'Mauri Washington is going to be a force in the 2026 college football season to set him up for a big draft process.

entry.title: Rams take offensive weapon instead of trading pick in 2025 re-draft
entry.content: The Rams could have upgraded their offense in a whole new way if they kept their 2025 first-round pick.

entry.title: New Report Reveals Reason A.J. Brown Was Frustrated With Jalen Hurts
entry.content: The New England Patriots traded for A.J. Brown this offseason, and his relationship with Jalen Hurts was reportedly one of the reasons he wanted out of Philadelphia.

entry.title: Reminder: macOS 27 won't work on Intel Macs
entry.content: You'll need to be running Apple silicon to use the latest operating system.

entry.title: The first iOS, iPadOS and macOS 27 developer betas are available now
entry.content: Developer betas are available now for Apple's upcoming suit of OSes.

entry.title: Siri AI for iPhones and iPads will be delayed indefinitely in the EU
entry.content: European Union and Chinese citizens will have to wait even longer before getting their hands on Apple's hotly-anticipated iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 with the new Siri.

entry.title: Vote for Republic's top 10 Arizona HS running backs for 2026 season
entry.content: The Republic has been highlighting top players by position to preview the 2026 Arizona high school football season. Here, it's running backs.

entry.title: Inter demand over €40 million to sell Yann Bisseck and why Bayern Munich move is possible
entry.content: Newly crowned Serie A champions Inter can now consider Yann Bisseck’s sale, if the right approach is made. The defender had a pretty good 2025/26 campaign but this isn’t enough for him to stay.La ...

entry.title: Apple expected to unveil new AI features at last developers conference
entry.content: Apple is expected to unveil new artificial intelligence features at its annual developers conference beginning Monday, which will be the last one featuring CEO Tim Cook before he turns his post over to John Ternus in September

entry.title: Trump’s NBA Finals Attendance Brings Added Security to Madison Square Garden
entry.content: The NYPD has banned Game 3 watch parties outside Madison Square Garden and fans face enhanced security and long waits to get inside.
